Established brands (BTU International, Entrepix, PR Hoffman). Hyperscaler-driven AI packaging demand validates it indirectly.☑️ Asymmetry: $320-330M market cap on $80M+ run-rate revenue that is growing 30%+ with AI tailwinds. Cash is around $24M. Forward earnings/revenues look cheap vs. AI peers too imo.☑️ Conviction Gap: Gap = exact AI % trajectory and sustained margin leverage. Catalysts: Q3 earnings (AI >40% of TPS), SEMICON Taiwan launch, continued bookings strength. Evidence already leaning right (31% growth).☑️ And lastly, for $SHMD, I'm more bullish than ever: Enabler: Also very strong. $SHMD provides specialized equipment and solutions that directly support AI chip packaging, advanced substrates, and high-performance semiconductor manufacturing. Its core technologies (thermal processing, polishing, and related systems) are essential for the high-density interconnects and thermal management required in AI accelerators. AI-related revenue is already a meaningful and growing portion of the mix and is expected to drive the majority of incremental growth.☑️ Leadership: Solid and aligned. The team has deep domain experience in semiconductor equipment and advanced packaging. Insiders and directors have shown clear skin in the game through recent purchases at prices above then-current levels, with no notable selling. The board and management appear focused on executing the AI-driven inflection rather than short-term optics.☑️ Revenue Quality: High quality with good visibility. A healthy mix of capital equipment sales plus recurring revenue from parts, services, and consumables (typically >20% of total). Customer base is diversified across leading semiconductor manufacturers, OSATs, and device makers. Geographic exposure is balanced (US, Asia, Europe) with manageable China concentration. Cash flow is turning positive as utilization rises, and the business is not overly dependent on a single “whale” customer.☑️ Forward Growth / Inflection: Clear and accelerating. Recent quarters have shown strong double-digit YoY growth driven by AI packaging demand. Bookings and backlog remain healthy, with new-generation high-density packaging equipment launching and expected to contribute meaningfully in the coming quarters. Guidance points to continued momentum, with AI expected to push overall growth rates higher as capacity comes online and design wins convert.☑️ Moat: Respectable. Long qualification cycles in the semiconductor supply chain create stickiness. Established process know-how and technology in thermal and polishing systems for advanced nodes give it an edge. Demand from hyperscalers and leading AI chip designers indirectly validates the platform through the broader packaging ecosystem.☑️ Asymmetry: Attractive. Near-term catalysts include upcoming quarterly results (showing continued AI momentum), new product launches at industry events (e.g., SEMICON), and further design-win conversions. text:
Wall Street is obsessed with AI chips.
But some investors are already looking at the next bottleneck.
@MichelleMakori explains – photonics uses light instead of electricity to move data between processors and servers. As AI demand explodes and copper constraints emerge, some believe optical networking could become critical infrastructure for the AI economy.
Is this where the next opportunity lies?
@MikeLeeStrategy weighs in on The Real Story with Michelle Makori.
Watch the full episode here: 
https://t.co/hUi9BTlEd6

Congratulations 🎉 to the winners of the 2026 Optica awards and medals! 

Find the full list of recipients in the OPN June issue: https://t.co/fiqyj8ejir

#honors #awards #optics #photonics https://t.co/qnI7q59Bcb

NTT is setting up a $440M+ fund to build next-gen optical AI infrastructure. Partners: SK Group (Korea) and Chunghwa Telecom (Taiwan).

The tech behind it is called IOWN. All-photonics network. No electrical signal conversion. Lower latency, lower power draw, higher bandwidth. NTT and Chunghwa already have a working link between Taipei and Tokyo running at 100Gbps with 34ms round-trip.

This is three countries pooling money to build AI infrastructure outside the US cloud stack. Most AI compute runs through AWS, Azure, and GCP. Asia is wiring its own backbone.

The real question: does AI's future run on American clouds or Asian light?

text:
My favourite asymmetries right now:  

> $AAOI - Raised FY26 guidance to $1.1B+ with demand outrunning supply into mid-2027 on the 800G and 1.6T ramp, then sold off 12% on the print. A raised guide into a sell-off is the setup.

>  $MRVL - Jensen called it the next trillion dollar company at Computex. $2B Nvidia stake, NVLink Fusion, and a genuine moat in custom silicon and silicon photonics. You can overcomplicate it and pretend you know more than the Godfather of AI, or you can just buy. 

> $KEEL - Amazon hiring in Moses Lake where Keel is building, first hyperscaler lease is the trigger. Panther Creek at 350MW is the real prize. This is just proof of execution. 

> $AMPG - relaunched its website and listed  $AMZN and $NVDA as customers for the first time. The Nvidia link traces to the AI-RAN work with Northeastern. Amazon is brand new. Management has already said new contracts are coming and would be announced soon.

> $NBIS - Do I really need to say anything

text:
$NVDA CEO Basically Claims That the Memory Shortages Aren't Ending Anytime Soon

"The memory market has recently been shrouded in negative rumors that "Nvidia has halved its use of memory modules in Vera Rubin server racks." However, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang came forward to refute the rumors, stating that 'there is no end in sight to the memory shortage," and announced that the company will purchase more memory from SK Hynix. The two companies will also work together to develop next-generation memory technology.

Analysts point out that Huang's remarks dispelled rumors of a sharp decline in Nvidia's memory usage, providing reassurance to memory manufacturers such as Nanya Technology (2408) and Winbond (2344) regarding their future prospects. Nvidia's plan to purchase more SK Hynix high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and collaborate on next-generation technology development will likely benefit Taiwanese manufacturers, particularly TSMC's subsidiary, Creative Labs (3443), which is working with SK Hynix to further develop HBM4-related ASIC-side IP.

Huang pointed out that the current demand in the AI ​​industry far exceeds the supply. The supply chain, from wafer manufacturing and advanced packaging to silicon photonics technology, is in a state of supply shortage. The demand is so large that it is difficult to meet. He predicts that this situation will continue for many years. In addition, AI infrastructure is still in a stage of rapid expansion, and there are no signs of the global data center investment boom cooling down.

When asked about the rumor that "Nvidia's Vera Rubin server rack memory module usage has been halved," Huang refuted it, emphasizing that "there is no end in sight to the memory shortage."

$DRAM $EWY

text:
LPKF Laser & Electronics SE (XETRA Germany: LPK) . They are a highly specialized German high-tech engineering and photonics company that builds precision, laser-based manufacturing systems.

Essentially, they create the complex laser machines that other companies use to build microchips, solar panels, and circuit boards.
The Quick Profile

Headquarters: Garbsen, Germany (near Hanover).
Founded: 1976 (they started out making mechanical tools for prototyping circuit boards before moving heavily into lasers).
Size: Publicly traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, with around 700 to 750 employees globally.

What Do They Actually Make?
Instead of making consumer electronics, LPKF operates behind the scenes in four main industrial sectors:

1/ Advanced Semiconductor Packaging (The Big Growth Area): They are currently making major waves in the microchip industry with a patented technology called LIDE (Laser Induced Deep Etching). As silicon chips hit their physical limits, the industry is shifting toward using glass as a base (interposer) for next-gen AI and high-performance computing chips. LIDE allows companies to micro-drill and structure glass without causing micro-cracks or defects.

2/ Electronics & Circuit Boards: They make laser depaneling systems (glorified, ultra-precise laser cutters) that slice fragile, flexible printed circuit boards (PCBs) out of larger sheets during manufacturing without damaging the tiny components. They also sell "Rapid Prototyping" benchtop lasers to universities and research labs so engineers can print custom circuit boards in-house.
3/ Laser Plastic Welding: They build machines that use lasers to fuse plastic parts together seamlessly. This is huge in medical devices and automotive manufacturing (like EV cooling systems)—especially with their recent "Absorbing-to-Absorbing" technology, which allows two dark, laser-absorbing plastics to be welded together without needing a transparent layer.
4/ Solar Energy: They develop laser scribing systems used to score and structure thin-film solar cells, increasing their efficiency. They are currently pivoting these machines to support next-generation perovskite solar cells.
1. Core Physics of the Disruption (LIDE® Technology)As an AI semiconductor specialist and physicist, the document analyzes the fundamental breakdown of organic packaging substrates when handling multi-GHz signals required by modern LLM compute clusters.The Problem: Conventional substrate materials experience high dielectric loss and mechanical warpage under the severe thermal fluctuations generated by massive tensor processing units.  The Solution: Moving to a glass core substrate. LPKF owns a patented chemical-physical process called LIDE® (Laser-Induced Deep Etching).  The Physics: The system utilizes precise, multi-photon laser absorption to alter the chemical microstructure of technical glasses (such as borosilicate) locally without injecting thermal shock or micro-cracks into the surrounding matrix. A subsequent selective wet-etching process attacks the modified glass at an accelerated selectivity ratio of up to 1:50.

They are a highly specialized German high-tech engineering and photonics company that builds precision, laser-based manufacturing systems. Essentially, they create the complex laser machines that other companies use to build microchips, solar panels, and circuit boards. The Quick Profile Headquarters: Garbsen, Germany (near Hanover). Founded: 1976 (they started out making mechanical tools for prototyping circuit boards before moving heavily into lasers). Size: Publicly traded on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, with around 700 to 750 employees globally. What Do They Actually Make? Instead of making consumer electronics, LPKF operates behind the scenes in four main industrial sectors: 1/ Advanced Semiconductor Packaging (The Big Growth Area): They are currently making major waves in the microchip industry with a patented technology called LIDE (Laser Induced Deep Etching). As silicon chips hit their physical limits, the industry is shifting toward using glass as a base (interposer) for next-gen AI and high-performance computing chips. LIDE allows companies to micro-drill and structure glass without causing micro-cracks or defects. 2/ Electronics & Circuit Boards: They make laser depaneling systems (glorified, ultra-precise laser cutters) that slice fragile, flexible printed circuit boards (PCBs) out of larger sheets during manufacturing without damaging the tiny components. They also sell "Rapid Prototyping" benchtop lasers to universities and research labs so engineers can print custom circuit boards in-house. 3/ Laser Plastic Welding: They build machines that use lasers to fuse plastic parts together seamlessly. This is huge in medical devices and automotive manufacturing (like EV cooling systems)—especially with their recent "Absorbing-to-Absorbing" technology, which allows two dark, laser-absorbing plastics to be welded together without needing a transparent layer. 4/ Solar Energy: They develop laser scribing systems used to score and structure thin-film solar cells, increasing their efficiency. They are currently pivoting these machines to support next-generation perovskite solar cells. 1. Core Physics of the Disruption (LIDE® Technology)As an AI semiconductor specialist and physicist, the document analyzes the fundamental breakdown of organic packaging substrates when handling multi-GHz signals required by modern LLM compute clusters.The Problem: Conventional substrate materials experience high dielectric loss and mechanical warpage under the severe thermal fluctuations generated by massive tensor processing units. The Solution: Moving to a glass core substrate. LPKF owns a patented chemical-physical process called LIDE® (Laser-Induced Deep Etching). The Physics: The system utilizes precise, multi-photon laser absorption to alter the chemical microstructure of technical glasses (such as borosilicate) locally without injecting thermal shock or micro-cracks into the surrounding matrix. A subsequent selective wet-etching process attacks the modified glass at an accelerated selectivity ratio of up to 1:50."}}}, "rest_id": "2064080630493700218", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "411", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
@mikealfred 

I rode $iren and $corz with you back in the corzq days. 

Bought the dream house and I’ve thanked you 20 times again for sharing you thesis. I still own a nice position size in $IREN AND when SLNH hit a $1.00 I picked 40,000 or so shares up. 

I’m Looking to keep the beach house and aim for the ranch - I landed on photonics as a replacement for copper in data centers going forward and upgrades to exsisting.

Debating between $KOPN (1 bill MC) who is integrated into dept of war w/ Anduril domestic production and is breaking into data centers with Micro LED - with gpu to gpu communication vs one of the larger established but not American made and 100B market cap 

Any thoughts this small asymmetric play or overall theme?

text:
Portfolio update week 19 - Road to 100K💰 

Balance: £14,150.01 💸 

YTD  - 24.4% 😢 

Current Positions ⬇️

$IREN - 300 Shares 

$MEMORYETF - £413.44

$PHOTONICSETF - £297.22

$NBIS - 1.055 Shares 

Big pullback on Friday but the port has responded well, $IREN back on its way to $60 is great to see 

I added £600 into the portfolio and started 3 new positions. I’ve created a memory and photonics pie that I plan to DCA into over the next few months as those are the 2 sectors along with DCs I am most bullish on 

I’ve also started a position in $NBIS however I am unsure if I am going to continue building it out or if I should just focus on the 2 pies I have built 🤔

text:
$SIVE delivers another very interesting move today, +13.45% (close 89.40 SEK), approaching the upper resistance zone marked at 85–90 SEK — after an aggressive rejection off the support zone at 74.50–78.50 SEK. 

The name continues to trade very extended, with a YTD return exceeding +2,000%, but that didn't stop me from taking exposure sub-40 SEK and DCA-ing at 60–65 SEK. 

The company has serious potential — recently discovered and now being accumulated by institutionals: JPMorgan disclosed a 5.25% stake in the company, its first-ever position in the stock. 

Having institutionals on our side confirms a lot. Imagine this name eventually listing on Nasdaq — access opens dramatically wider for investment funds and everyone who has missed the move so far. 

There are plenty of bearish theories that say it'll round-trip back to where it came from, but I'm very satisfied with the decision so far and will hold it for a longer period until I'm satisfied with the gain — currently +160%+, with higher targets in mind. 

The photonics names have momentum again — they keep getting bought after every dip, which suggests the move can continue.

text:
"North America sales halved purely from US defense budget approvals slipping into H2"

--> Sivers' photonics division doesn't have material U.S. defense photonics revenue to lose. Their photonics business is a LiDAR/CPO play - automotive and datacom, not defense radar or military satcom. The CHIPS Act award applied to their wireless/RF division, not photonics. Photonics revenue was DOWN 32% from last year. 

--> The opportunity pipeline is meaningless word salad. 

--> Speaking of dilution, we happen to know exactly when and where the next round is! On June 15, at their Annual General Meeting, the board will pass a 15% dilution round. 

If you absolutely must buy into this amazing "growth" story, probably wait until that passes. 

Good luck!

text:
Everything about my investing strategy, capital, and performance since I started investing a year and a half ago.

My investing journey

I started 2025 with around $3.5k, mostly index fund maxxxing.

Then I got wrecked by the Trump market volatility and entered 2026 with around $18.5k and a -5% performance.

Six months later, my portfolio is sitting at roughly $46k . Around $30k came from monthly DCA, but the rest came from performance.

My current goal is to reach $100k by the end of the year while keeping my DCA much lower than in year one. I’m no longer trying to save every penny. Instead, I’m focusing on two things : building a high-conviction bull-growth investing strategy and increasing my income.

I believe the AI supercycle, aka stock market in easy mode, still has a few years left before we return to hard mode.

My strategy is simple : identify businesses positioned to capture a meaningful share of the AI infrastructure buildout, especially in capital-intensive markets where contracts, scale, and execution can quickly reprice a company.

The goal is to maximize returns through short to medium-term asymmetric bets, while holding my highest-conviction names as long as the thesis continues to play out.

Current holdings and 2026 price targets

$IREN : End-of-year PT: $140 (+237%)

IREN is my largest position, representing around 73% of my portfolio.

The stock has underperformed over the last six months, but my conviction remains very high. I believe the next wave of contracts, improved economics, and continued scaling could push the stock significantly higher.

This is my highest-conviction AI infrastructure bet. I plan to hold and watch the company scale until at least 2030, as long as the thesis keeps playing out.

$NUAI : End-of-year PT : $15 (+300%)

NUAI represents around 12% of my portfolio.

The company has built a very strong team and is positioning itself for a potential hyperscaler deal around its 650MW Texas campus.

This single event could potentially double the stock’s value, especially considering the company is currently valued at around $500M .

$SIVE : End-of-year PT : 500 SEK (+500%)

Sivers represents around 10% of my portfolio.

This is a much more speculative position, but the upside could be massive. If Sivers manages to capture a meaningful share of future photonics demand, I don’t see how the company stays below a $3B market cap.

The risk is high, but so is the potential reward.

$AMPG : End-of-year PT : $40 (+500%)

AMPG represents around 5% of my portfolio.

This is my moonshot / for-fun position, built from profits made on a recent swing trade. I haven’t done as much research on this company as I have on my core positions, but the growth potential is serious.

The company could become increasingly involved in contracts with hyperscale clients, which makes it an interesting high-risk, high-reward bet.

Final thoughts

My portfolio is extremely concentrated and clearly not built for everyone.

But I’m young, I have high risk tolerance, and I believe this market still offers many asymmetric opportunities for investors willing to do their D.D and not afraid of volatility.

The objective is to aggressively compound capital during what I believe is the biggest infrastructure buildouts of the century.

My investing journey I started 2025 with around $3.5k, mostly index fund maxxxing. Then I got wrecked by the Trump market volatility and entered 2026 with around $18.5k and a -5% performance. Six months later, my portfolio is sitting at roughly $46k . Around $30k came from monthly DCA, but the rest came from performance. My current goal is to reach $100k by the end of the year while keeping my DCA much lower than in year one. I’m no longer trying to save every penny. Instead, I’m focusing on two things : building a high-conviction bull-growth investing strategy and increasing my income. I believe the AI supercycle, aka stock market in easy mode, still has a few years left before we return to hard mode. My strategy is simple : identify businesses positioned to capture a meaningful share of the AI infrastructure buildout, especially in capital-intensive markets where contracts, scale, and execution can quickly reprice a company. The goal is to maximize returns through short to medium-term asymmetric bets, while holding my highest-conviction names as long as the thesis continues to play out. Current holdings and 2026 price targets $IREN : End-of-year PT: $140 (+237%) IREN is my largest position, representing around 73% of my portfolio. The stock has underperformed over the last six months, but my conviction remains very high. I believe the next wave of contracts, improved economics, and continued scaling could push the stock significantly higher. This is my highest-conviction AI infrastructure bet. I plan to hold and watch the company scale until at least 2030, as long as the thesis keeps playing out. $NUAI : End-of-year PT : $15 (+300%) NUAI represents around 12% of my portfolio. The company has built a very strong team and is positioning itself for a potential hyperscaler deal around its 650MW Texas campus. This single event could potentially double the stock’s value, especially considering the company is currently valued at around $500M . $SIVE : End-of-year PT : 500 SEK (+500%) Sivers represents around 10% of my portfolio. This is a much more speculative position, but the upside could be massive. If Sivers manages to capture a meaningful share of future photonics demand, I don’t see how the company stays below a $3B market cap. The risk is high, but so is the potential reward. $AMPG : End-of-year PT : $40 (+500%) AMPG represents around 5% of my portfolio. This is my moonshot / for-fun position, built from profits made on a recent swing trade. I haven’t done as much research on this company as I have on my core positions, but the growth potential is serious. The company could become increasingly involved in contracts with hyperscale clients, which makes it an interesting high-risk, high-reward bet. Final thoughts My portfolio is extremely concentrated and clearly not built for everyone. But I’m young, I have high risk tolerance, and I believe this market still offers many asymmetric opportunities for investors willing to do their D.D and not afraid of volatility. The objective is to aggressively compound capital during what I believe is the biggest infrastructure buildouts of the century."}}}, "rest_id": "2065903808245317656", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "116", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

$POET is one I’m watching here.

It’s a photonics / optical interconnect name tied to AI data center connectivity, basically the type of tech needed to move data faster and more efficiently.

After a quick rally, POET pulled back into key support and held.

On the higher timeframe, this still looks like a bull flag.

Price bounced off the Ichimoku cloud and is now trying to reclaim the fast momentum cloud.

If it breaks out of this flag, this could set up for another leg higher.

text: @CKCapitalxx whos saying that lol. everyone on my feed sounds like they want to liquidate their 401k to ape in on more photonics

𝑻𝒉𝒆 𝒄𝒉𝒊𝒑𝒔 𝒘𝒆𝒓𝒆 𝒋𝒖𝒔𝒕 𝒄𝒉𝒂𝒑𝒕𝒆𝒓 𝒐𝒏𝒆.

The real constraints on scaling AI are no longer measured in teraflops alone. They’re measured in megawatts you can actually deliver, photons you can move at rack scale, liquid you can pump without melting the floor, and platforms that turn raw infrastructure into deployed, governed intelligence.

Jensen has been clear for months: the handoff is happening. Copper has limits. Power is the new gate. Cooling density is non-negotiable. The companies securing these layers with long-term contracts and balance-sheet capital are building durable positions.

Here’s how the 2026 AI infrastructure stack breaks down from silicon to intelligence:

LAYER 1: SILICON FOUNDATION  
🟠 $TSM  — The advanced foundry and packaging backbone (CoWoS and beyond). This is the capacity gate for the highest-performance AI chips. Everything upstream starts here.

LAYER 2: AI COMPUTE  
🟠 $NVDA  — Still the clear leader in GPUs and full AI systems/racks. The ecosystem investments and rack-level selling show they understand the game has moved beyond individual chips.

LAYER 3: SERVERS & RACK INTEGRATION  
🔵 $SMCI — Building dense, liquid-ready AI server and rack systems that hyperscalers deploy at scale. Direct volume beneficiary of the buildout.

LAYER 4: NETWORKING & HIGH-SPEED INTERCONNECT  
🔵 $MRVL  — Switching silicon, DSPs, and aggressive silicon photonics push via Celestial AI. Essential for scaling clusters beyond single racks.  
🔵 $AAOI  — Datacom optics leader with explosive data center revenue growth and a major 800G/1.6T order backlog. Scaling domestic capacity while supply chain security matters.

LAYER 5: PHOTONICS & OPTICS TRANSITION  
The physics is clear, copper hits the wall on bandwidth density and power. Photonics moves from “nice to have” to required for the next jump in cluster scale.  
🔵 $LITE (Lumentum) — Leading provider of high-speed lasers, EMLs, and optical components with a major NVIDIA strategic investment and multi-billion dollar purchase commitments. Scaling US manufacturing for AI data center optics demand.

LAYER 6: POWER: THE BINDING CONSTRAINT  
🟠 $VST — Signing multi-decade power purchase agreements directly with hyperscalers. Nuclear uprates and reliable generation capacity are becoming strategic assets.  
🔵 $IREN  — Renewable-powered data centers executing a clear pivot into AI/HPC cloud. Large-scale sites with secured power and flexible capacity. 5.8 GW-scale pipeline building.

LAYER 7: COOLING & THERMAL MANAGEMENT  
🔵 $VRT  — Liquid cooling systems are moving from pilot to must-have for high-density AI racks. Record backlog and order growth as power density per rack climbs.

LAYER 8: CLOUD & DATA CENTER PLATFORMS  
🔵 $ORCL  — Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is seeing massive AI-driven demand. Major capex ramp, OpenAI/Stargate ecosystem work, and enormous remaining performance obligations.  
🔵 $NBIS  — AI infrastructure specialist rapidly scaling contracted capacity (multi-GW targets). Major hyperscaler deals are driving strong ARR acceleration.

LAYER 9: THE INTELLIGENCE LAYER  
🔵 $PLTR  — Palantir’s AIP platform turns physical infrastructure plus enterprise data into production-grade, governed AI applications. U.S. commercial momentum is accelerating sharply.

Capital is already voting with real money: direct hyperscaler power deals, NVIDIA’s multi-billion dollar optics investments, pre-payments for capacity years out, and software platforms seeing accelerating commercial traction.

The honest counter-thesis is that real valuations across parts of the stack are elevated, power timelines can slip, cooling and photonics tech is still maturing in some deployments. Software ROI takes time to prove at scale. One digestive quarter can pressure the whole chain. But the structural, multi-year spend from the largest balance sheets in tech is not a narrative. It’s signed contracts and capex plans.

The market has aggressively priced the obvious chip layer. The asymmetry sits in the scarce layers everything else depends on.

Own the full stack. Not just the logo on the GPU.

Bookmark this. Then drop which layer you think moves first or which one I’m underweighting.

Tag the person who’s still only buying NVDA and calling it a diversified AI portfolio.

text:
The Copper-to-Light Shift: Why Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVE is sitting right in the middle of the perfect AI storm

We’re standing at a shift that reminds me strongly of when the world moved from copper to fiber in broadband. Back then, many smart people looked at the situation and said “this is good enough – we don’t need to change everything.” History proved them wrong in a spectacular way. Fiber didn’t just improve the internet – it completely unlocked streaming, cloud computing, social media, and the entire digital society we take for granted today.

Now the same story is repeating itself, only this time the stakes are dramatically higher.

Copper has reached its physical and economic limit.

AI is growing at a pace we’ve never seen. Massive clusters with tens of thousands – soon hundreds of thousands – of GPUs that all need to communicate with each other at extreme speeds, constantly. Moore’s Law has kept delivering smaller, faster and more power-efficient transistors on the chips themselves, but the interconnects – the actual nervous system that lets all these chips talk to one another – are stuck in the past. Copper simply can’t handle it anymore. We see signal degradation over distance, enormous heat generation, sky-high energy consumption, and physical scaling problems that get worse with every new generation of AI. At the same time, the copper price itself is surging because of AI demand and global electrification. Building data centers the old way isn’t just becoming technically challenging anymore – it’s becoming economically absurd.

So what’s the alternative?

Honestly, there aren’t many good ones left.

We have to move to light.

Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) – where the optics and lasers are placed right next to the chips – changes the game completely. Dramatically lower power consumption, vastly higher bandwidth, and scalability that copper can never match. The biggest hyperscalers and chip companies are already pouring billions into this technology. This isn’t a nice-to-have. It’s a survival requirement for the next generation of AI. Without this shift we will simply hit a hard wall in how far AI can advance.

Just like fiber laid the foundation for today’s digital world, CPO and photonics in general will become the invisible backbone of tomorrow’s AI infrastructure. There is no realistic way around it.

And sitting right in the middle of this perfect storm is Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVEF.

At the very heart of the CPO revolution you need reliable, high-performance laser light sources that can feed these new optical engines efficiently. That is exactly Sivers’ strongest area with their advanced DFB laser arrays. They are already partnering closely with the companies that are building the actual next-generation infrastructure: $GFS and their SCALE CPO platform (targeting a $25 billion pluggable optics market by 2030), $POET, O-Net, $ENA.NE and several others for scalable External Laser Source solutions. 

They combine this with an asset-light model that gives them speed and flexibility to scale without massive capital expenditure, a clear technical edge in customized lasers, proven working demos together with players like Ayar Labs, and natural exposure to AI data centers, SATCOM and defense – markets that all need precisely this kind of photonics.

The broader shift from copper to light in AI infrastructure is heading toward tens of billions of dollars in the coming years. Sivers is positioned where the critical technology meets exploding demand. 

During CMD 2025, CEO Vickram Vathulya highlighted from customer conversations that Sivers seems to be the only ones capable of providing laser arrays in the exact formats and power levels that customers need for these new architectures. A few others do laser arrays, but at much lower power levels.

CTO Andrew McKee added even more color at the same event: 2027 is the target for volume production, with qualification starting this year and pilot ramp-up in 2026. Looking at 2028 forecasts of 19 million AI GPU shipments, and typical architectures needing 5–16 laser sources per GPU, we’re talking hundreds of millions of laser sources per year. An extremely large and exciting addressable market as this rolls out in the data centers.

This isn’t just another incremental upgrade. It’s a fundamental infrastructure transformation.

The ones who understand this early will see why photonics is becoming one of the clearest and strongest tailwinds in the entire AI value chain.

$SIVE feels extremely well positioned.

Back then, many smart people looked at the situation and said “this is good enough – we don’t need to change everything.” History proved them wrong in a spectacular way. Fiber didn’t just improve the internet – it completely unlocked streaming, cloud computing, social media, and the entire digital society we take for granted today. Now the same story is repeating itself, only this time the stakes are dramatically higher. Copper has reached its physical and economic limit. AI is growing at a pace we’ve never seen. Massive clusters with tens of thousands – soon hundreds of thousands – of GPUs that all need to communicate with each other at extreme speeds, constantly. Moore’s Law has kept delivering smaller, faster and more power-efficient transistors on the chips themselves, but the interconnects – the actual nervous system that lets all these chips talk to one another – are stuck in the past. Copper simply can’t handle it anymore. We see signal degradation over distance, enormous heat generation, sky-high energy consumption, and physical scaling problems that get worse with every new generation of AI. At the same time, the copper price itself is surging because of AI demand and global electrification. Building data centers the old way isn’t just becoming technically challenging anymore – it’s becoming economically absurd. So what’s the alternative? Honestly, there aren’t many good ones left. We have to move to light. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) – where the optics and lasers are placed right next to the chips – changes the game completely. Dramatically lower power consumption, vastly higher bandwidth, and scalability that copper can never match. The biggest hyperscalers and chip companies are already pouring billions into this technology. This isn’t a nice-to-have. It’s a survival requirement for the next generation of AI. Without this shift we will simply hit a hard wall in how far AI can advance. Just like fiber laid the foundation for today’s digital world, CPO and photonics in general will become the invisible backbone of tomorrow’s AI infrastructure. There is no realistic way around it. And sitting right in the middle of this perfect storm is Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVEF. At the very heart of the CPO revolution you need reliable, high-performance laser light sources that can feed these new optical engines efficiently. That is exactly Sivers’ strongest area with their advanced DFB laser arrays. They are already partnering closely with the companies that are building the actual next-generation infrastructure: $GFS and their SCALE CPO platform (targeting a $25 billion pluggable optics market by 2030), $POET, O-Net, $ENA.NE and several others for scalable External Laser Source solutions. They combine this with an asset-light model that gives them speed and flexibility to scale without massive capital expenditure, a clear technical edge in customized lasers, proven working demos together with players like Ayar Labs, and natural exposure to AI data centers, SATCOM and defense – markets that all need precisely this kind of photonics. The broader shift from copper to light in AI infrastructure is heading toward tens of billions of dollars in the coming years. Sivers is positioned where the critical technology meets exploding demand. During CMD 2025, CEO Vickram Vathulya highlighted from customer conversations that Sivers seems to be the only ones capable of providing laser arrays in the exact formats and power levels that customers need for these new architectures. A few others do laser arrays, but at much lower power levels. CTO Andrew McKee added even more color at the same event: 2027 is the target for volume production, with qualification starting this year and pilot ramp-up in 2026. Looking at 2028 forecasts of 19 million AI GPU shipments, and typical architectures needing 5–16 laser sources per GPU, we’re talking hundreds of millions of laser sources per year. An extremely large and exciting addressable market as this rolls out in the data centers. This isn’t just another incremental upgrade. It’s a fundamental infrastructure transformation. The ones who understand this early will see why photonics is becoming one of the clearest and strongest tailwinds in the entire AI value chain. $SIVE feels extremely well positioned."}}}, "rest_id": "2065889424605618466", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "11167", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Most people still don’t see it. Photonics is where Memory was a year ago. That’s why we’re still VERY early. The pattern is clear if you’ve been watching this AI super cycle unfold since 2022. > Chips kicked everything off after ChatGPT dropped in late 2022/early 2023. $NVDA went nuclear with Hopper H100s, and then Blackwell came. $AMD started carving out real share in GPUs and custom ASICs. > Memory followed with a lag. Mid-2024 onward, HBM went into massive shortage as AI training and inference sucked up capacity. That pulled DRAM and NAND higher too. Micron $MU, SK Hynix, Samsung and other memory players delivered strong re-ratings as pricing power returned. > Now in mid-2026, memory is deep in its prolonged supercycle - sold out for years ahead (confirmed by Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra). Chips are in a more mature phase, still growing strong with 1:1 CPU to GPU ratios as AI agents scale, and will sustain into the second half of this decade as Lisa Su says compute capacity will 100x by 2030. But what’s next? Photonics, folks! - AI clusters are suffering from constraints related to power, heat, latency and bandwidth. Optics solves this. That is why the next MAJOR leg is clearly SiPh and CPO. - 1.6T pluggables are ramping, early CPO deployments are starting but nowhere near to the scale they will reach because real mass adoption and deep in-package integration will come in 2027-2028+. - Goldman Sach predicts TAM will explode 10x by the end of this decade. To better understand this, if one divides it into 3 common phases across the supercycles (Early, Acceleration, Maturity), we see that: - Chips are clearly in Phase 3 - Memory is in Phase 2/3 - Photonics is very early in Phase 2 🔥 The AI buildout keeps shifting to the next bottleneck because the world needs more compute than ever before. This isn’t slowing down anytime soon! BULLISH seems like a bearish word now."}}}, "rest_id": "2065832912956641690", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "64069", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2065869934240866674", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7658", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Je crois qu’on a réveillé un monstre avec $VECO 

La dernière news Ennostar est beaucoup plus importante qu’elle en a l’air

>Ennostar vient de qualifier le nouveau LUMINA+ MOCVD de Veeco pour la production high-volume d’applications optoélectroniques avancées

Ce n’est pas juste une évaluation, c’est une qualification production

Et ça ajoute une troisième jambe à la thèse $VECO qu'on a ennoncé dans les précédents posts et que je vais vous refaire car vous êtes des petit flemmards

1. Silicon photonics/InP lasers

>Lors de ses derniers earnings qui semblaient être décevants, lors du call Veeco a  annoncé plus de $250M de commandes pour ses outils Spector, Lumina et WaferEtch destinés à la fabrication de lasers InP.

>Ces lasers sont critiques pour les transceivers 800G / 1.6T utilisés dans les data centers hyperscale et le narratif adore ce produit

Livraisons en 2026 -> accélération forte en 2027

2. Advanced logic/NSA500

>Veeco a reçu une commande de suivi pour son système NSA500 après validation par un client logic avancé (cf dernier post).

>Ils ont aussi envoyé un NSA500 à un troisième client advanced logic pour évaluation, avec potentiel de premières commandes HVM en 2027.

>Le marché commence à comprendre que le laser annealing devient critique pour les architectures avancées, les structures 3D, les nouveaux nœuds et les budgets thermiques ultra-serrés.

3. LUMINA+/MOCVD/As-P/microLED

>La qualification par Ennostar valide la nouvelle plateforme LUMINA+ pour la production.

>C’est important parce que Veeco ne joue pas seulement l’IA via la photonique.

>Ils jouent aussi les compound semiconductors, l’optoélectronique, les microLED, le biosensing, l’automotive et les communications optiques.

Donc la thèse $VECO n’est plus seulement :

“la petite boîte d’équipements semi”.

C’est plutôt :

“LE fournisseur d’outils critiques sur plusieurs bottlenecks de la prochaine décennie”.

Le bull case devient très intéressant.

Aujourd’hui, $VECO vaut environ $4.7B de market cap.

Si la boîte passe de $750M-$800M de revenus à $1.5B-$1.8B d’ici 2030, avec un meilleur mix semi et des marges qui montent, le titre peut se revaloriser beaucoup plus haut (logique)

À 8x-9x sales, on arrive déjà vers $12B-$16B de market cap.

Soit environ $200-$265 par action.

Dans un scénario très bull, avec $2B+ de revenus, plusieurs clients HVM sur NSA500, adoption plus large de LUMINA+ et bottleneck durable sur InP/silicon photonics, on peut commencer à parler de $300+.

Bear case évident :

Les évaluations peuvent glisser, les commandes  peuvent prendre du temps, le cycle capex semi peut ralentir, et la concurrence reste réelle.

Je continue cependant à être haussier sur ce dossier

text:
QUANTUM looks interesting at this point. 

$IONQ → Trapped-ion leader. Best-in-class qubit fidelity. Customers include Airbus, AstraZeneca, Hyundai. 256-qubit demo targeted 2026. The institutional-grade pure-play.

$RGTI → Superconducting quantum systems. Highest beta in the sector. When quantum runs, $RGTI moves violently. Active momentum name right now.

$QBTS → D-Wave Quantum. Annealing-based architecture. Most commercially de-risked pure-play — already generating optimization revenue with real enterprise clients.

$QUBT → Photonic + room-temperature quantum. Q1 2026 revenue up from $39K → $3.7M YoY. Acquired Luminar Semiconductor for $110M. Vertically integrated photonics + quantum platform taking shape. Executing quietly.

$INFQ → Neutral-atom quantum + sensing. One of the least-covered names in the sector. Neutral-atom architecture is gaining credibility as a scalable path to fault tolerance. Early but worth watching.

$ARQQ → Quantum encryption and post-quantum cybersecurity. The national security angle. As quantum breaks classical encryption — this becomes critical infrastructure.

$LAES → Quantum-resistant cybersecurity chips. Hardware-level protection against quantum decryption. Defense + enterprise security tailwind.

Every Play. One Post. PURE-PLAY QUANTUM $IONQ → Trapped-ion leader. Best-in-class qubit fidelity. Customers include Airbus, AstraZeneca, Hyundai. 256-qubit demo targeted 2026. The institutional-grade pure-play. $RGTI → Superconducting quantum systems. Highest beta in the sector. When quantum runs, $RGTI moves violently. Active momentum name right now. $QBTS → D-Wave Quantum. Annealing-based architecture. Most commercially de-risked pure-play — already generating optimization revenue with real enterprise clients. $QUBT → Photonic + room-temperature quantum. Q1 2026 revenue up from $39K → $3.7M YoY. Acquired Luminar Semiconductor for $110M. Vertically integrated photonics + quantum platform taking shape. Executing quietly. $INFQ → Neutral-atom quantum + sensing. One of the least-covered names in the sector. Neutral-atom architecture is gaining credibility as a scalable path to fault tolerance. Early but worth watching. $ARQQ → Quantum encryption and post-quantum cybersecurity. The national security angle. As quantum breaks classical encryption — this becomes critical infrastructure. $LAES → Quantum-resistant cybersecurity chips. Hardware-level protection against quantum decryption. Defense + enterprise security tailwind. BIG TECH $IBM → Most mature public quantum roadmap. 1000+ qubit processors live. Fault-tolerant systems targeted ~2029. Every enterprise quantum conversation starts here. $GOOGL → Willow chip demonstrated a landmark quantum error correction milestone. Google doesn’t lose science races. This is a long-term compounder with quantum upside baked in. $MSFT → Topological qubit breakthrough. Azure Quantum as the monetization layer. Full-stack quantum integrator play for the enterprise cloud era. $AMZN → AWS Braket quantum cloud. Positioned as the access layer for quantum-as-a-service. Already charging enterprises for quantum compute access today. $NVDA → Quantum-AI software stack integration. CUDA for quantum is the longer-term thesis. $NVDA doesn’t need to win quantum — it needs to be the layer everything runs on top of. $INTC → Silicon-spin qubit research. The most scalable long-term architecture thesis — leveraging existing CMOS manufacturing. Slow, but strategically important. $HON → Majority stake in Quantinuum — the most commercially advanced quantum hardware + software company currently private. When Quantinuum IPOs, $HON re-rates hard. $BAH → Booz Allen Hamilton. Deep in U.S. government quantum programs. Every federal quantum contract flows through firms like this. The picks-and-shovels of government quantum. SEMICONDUCTOR & INFRASTRUCTURE $GFS → GlobalFoundries. Quantum chip manufacturing capabilities. As quantum hardware scales, fab demand follows. $MU → Memory + quantum infrastructure angle. Quantum systems require extreme classical compute support — $MU sits in that stack. $AMD → HPC + quantum research ecosystem. High-performance classical compute is the co-processor to every near-term quantum system. $TSM → TSMC. Advanced fabrication is the foundation of every quantum chip roadmap. No quantum at scale without $TSM. $ASML → EUV lithography critical for next-generation quantum chip manufacturing. The irreplaceable chokepoint in advanced semiconductor production. QUANTUM NETWORKING / OPTICAL / SECURITY $CIEN → Optical networking backbone + quantum networking research. Quantum communication requires ultra-low-noise optical infrastructure — $CIEN is already there. $NOK → Nokia building quantum-safe telecom infrastructure. Nation-state cyber threats are accelerating the quantum-safe network upgrade cycle. $LITE → Photonics and optical infrastructure. Quantum and photonics are deeply intertwined. $AAOI → Optical connectivity. Riding both the AI and quantum photonics buildout simultaneously. $COHR → Photonics + laser systems. Lasers are fundamental to trapped-ion and photonic quantum architectures."}}}, "rest_id": "2058543628906029167", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "939288", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2066234093457584520", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "14502", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Plus the lab which I am most close to (mechatronics + advanced manufacturing lab) that includes 3D printer, CNC milling, machining, electro-discharge machine, robotics lab etc.
But to gain college funding will take time and will need me to enroll in either PhD programme or have something that even Professors are motivated towards (like publishing a research paper).
Just know that I am very interested im this sort of thing, especially building a new type of motor

text:
Guys we need to talk about $AAOI...

$AAOI is one of the most controversial stocks in the world and I enjoy the disagreement.

Pretty cool that's it up 3x since I went long with many thousands of investors riding it with me up some portion of that gain on @joinautopilot.

But what's cooler is that I think it's still asymmetrical here.

So let's break it down and figure out where we're at $170/share today.

Management argues they can get to $471M in monthly transceiver revenue by Q2 2027.

If you're new to photonics, this is because transceivers are one of the hottest commodities in data centers right now (they translate electricity to light) and 3 hyperscalers are working with $AAOI on this.

At management's 40% gross margin target, that's ~$5.6B in annualized revenue. Run past opex, interest, tax, dilution, we might land around $14-15 in EPS on that run rate.

That's a 2028 number though because 2027 is the ramp mid year, so let's call it bull case $9 of EPS in 2027.

So at $170/share right now, on these calcs, you're paying 19x 2027 earnings, falling dramatically to 12x for 2028.

Don't trust management? Fair. Haircut both the revenue and the margin target by 30%, let's say we get to $4B at a 28% gross margin instead of $5.6B at 40%.

You still land near ~$6 in EPS, just over the analyst mean estimate for 2027E. That's 28x at $170, still WAY cheaper than $LITE and $COHR trade right now.

$LITE is at a 50x NTM forward PE per Fiscal AI.

text:
The restrictions on Anthropics Fable 5 and Mythos 5 aren’t bearish

If anything, they’re proof that AI has become strategic national infrastructure
Governments don’t restrict technologies they view as unimportant

The bigger takeaway is what happens next 

The UAE, France etc and others will accelerate sovereign AI initiatives
More domestic compute, more local models = data centers
No country wants to depend on infrastructure that can be switched off by another jurisdiction overnight

That means more demand across the entire AI supply chain

Compute. Chips. Lasers. Fiber. Power. Cooling.

The picks and shovels providers don’t care whose flag is flying over the data center

There may be some short term volatility, but long term this looks like another tailwind for the infrastructure layer

The AI buildout isn’t slowing down. It’s becoming more fragmented, more localized, and ultimately larger

The real bottlenecks remain the same
lasers, InP substrates, silicon photonics, power delivery, and fiber connectivity

Compute: $NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Optics: $AAOI $SIVE $COHR $LITE $AXTI $AIXA $SOI $FOCI $TSEM $NCI

Power: $NVTS $AOSL $POWI

Sovereign Compute/DC: $NBIS $GLW

More sovereign clusters means more demand chasing the same constrained supply chain

The supercycle remains intact

I have positions in many of these names. NFA.

text:
Jensen Huang told a room of global investors that AI is not one industry. It is five stacked on top of each other. Most people are investing in layer four and ignoring layers one through three entirely.

He called it the five-layer cake.

Layer one is energy. Jensen said this is the single greatest opportunity for the energy industry in a hundred years.

The first time in a century that the grid in most countries can actually attract serious capital. Nuclear, solar, wind, hydrogen, it does not matter what form. If it produces energy, it gets funded. Siemens, GE Vernova, Mitsubishi. That is why they are all doing so well right now.

Layer two is chips, computers, networking, and silicon photonics. Everything that processes the intelligence.

Layer three is infrastructure. Land, power, buildings, data center operations. Every single one in short supply today.

Layer four is the model layer. OpenAI, Anthropic. The layer everyone talks about.

Layer five is applications. Every startup applying AI to financial services, legal, healthcare, logistics, transportation. Last year alone, a hundred billion dollars of venture capital went into this layer. The single largest VC year in the history of humanity.

Then he said the number that stopped me cold.

We are putting one trillion dollars into this five-layer cake this year. That sounds enormous. Jensen thinks the AI industry will eventually run at twenty trillion dollars per year.

We are one trillion in of a twenty trillion dollar per year ecosystem.

Most people watching AI are staring at layer four. Jensen was describing layers one through five as a single compounding system where every layer feeds the one above it.

The people who understand that will invest differently than the people who do not.

text:
Most of this map is noise to the average investor.

But one name is quietly sitting on the layer everything else depends on, and almost nobody sees it. 

That name is $AMPG.

The one that I think will do a parabolic move like $SIVE or $AAOI.

Let me tell you the whole story.

Look at where it sits: Connectivity & RF.

The re-shored, certified domestic alternative for 5G, SATCOM and defense.

One name in its lane.

Here's why that lane is the one almost nobody is pricing correctly.

Look at every other layer on this list. Photonics. Compute. Physical AI. Drones. Space. Energy.

Every single one of them, at some point, has to move its signal somewhere.

Data has to travel.

And the layer that moves it through the air is RF, the radio. It's the connective tissue under the entire map.

No radio, nothing else talks to anything.

Now the problem that makes this a thesis and not just a product.

America does not make its own radios.

The companies that build the RF backbone of modern networks are all foreign: Nokia (Finland), Ericsson (Sweden), Samsung (Korea).

The Chinese ones, Huawei and ZTE, are banned outright on national-security grounds.

So the most powerful country on Earth, about to wire its economy, its defense and its AI into a wireless network, depends on other countries for the physical layer it runs on.

That is a strategic vulnerability.

Washington knows it.

That's the gap $AMPG fills.

AmpliTech is the only American company that designs and commercializes a 64T64R Massive MIMO O-RAN radio.

That's the highest-capacity radio configuration in the modern stack, and it's the physical hardware that open AI-RAN runs on.

Not the only one on Earth, Nokia and Ericsson make them too.

The only American one.

In a decade defined by re-shoring critical tech, that single word, American, is the whole point.

And this isn't a pitch deck.

It's already real.

It's deployed at Telus, a Tier-1 North American carrier, running on live Open RAN sites alongside Samsung.

It's a Strategic Partner in Open6G, the wireless hub funded by the US Department of Defense and run by Northeastern, sitting in the top partner tier right next to NVIDIA, Dell and Qualcomm.

Its radio was the physical unit in the world's first open-source Massive MIMO AI-RAN demo, running with NVIDIA's Aerial software.

And it was the only American-designed 64T64R radio to pass multi-vendor interoperability at the O-RAN ALLIANCE Global PlugFest.

Then look at who shows up on its customer wall: NVIDIA, Amazon, IBM, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, L3Harris, NASA.

You do not land defense primes by accident.

Those relationships take years of qualification before you're even in the room.

That's a moat you can't fake.

Now the fundamentals, because a thesis needs a business under it. 48% gross margins, up from 33%.

Debt-free.

$50M revenue guidance for the year (and they hit their prior guide, they don't have a habit of underdelivering).

And managament promised even more.

Real backlog, real LOIs.

This is a company that already makes money doing this, today, with the radio.

And stacked on top, for free, two pieces of optionality.

AI-RAN, where towers become intelligent edge nodes, the demo with NVIDIA points at exactly where this goes.

And quantum, where AMPG makes the cryogenic amplifiers superconducting quantum computers need for qubit readout (it's delivered proof-of-concept units to names like IBM and Google).

I'll be honest about both: optionality, not the core thesis. Cheap call options on top of a real business, not the reason to own it.

Here's the honest framing that actually makes this stronger, not weaker.

$AMPG is not a chokepoint nobody can replace.

AI runs without it.

Other radio makers exist.

I won't pretend it's irreplaceable, because it isn't.

What it is, is the sovereign alternative.

The American option in a layer the US increasingly refuses to outsource

That's a strategic preference backed by policy and funding, not a technical monopoly.

And strategically favored can re-rate a sub-$1B company just as hard as technically indispensable can.

And the timing isn't subtle.

The US just restricted its most advanced AI models from all foreign nationals, even allies.

When a country starts walling off its critical tech from its own friends, it tells you exactly how it's going to treat the physical layer its AI economy runs on.

It's going to want that made at home.

So in a map full of chokepoints and physical inputs, $AMPG is the layer that moves the signal, re-shored, certified, and American.

The screens get the attention.

The infrastructure gets the returns.

Not financial advice. I'm long $AMPG. DYOR. 📡

But one name is quietly sitting on the layer everything else depends on, and almost nobody sees it. 

That name is $AMPG.

The one that I think will do a parabolic move like $SIVE or $AAOI.

Let me tell you the whole story.

Look at where it sits: Connectivity & RF.

The re-shored, certified domestic alternative for 5G, SATCOM and defense.

One name in its lane.

Here's why that lane is the one almost nobody is pricing correctly.

Look at every other layer on this list. Photonics. Compute. Physical AI. Drones. Space. Energy.

Every single one of them, at some point, has to move its signal somewhere.

Data has to travel.

And the layer that moves it through the air is RF, the radio. It's the connective tissue under the entire map.

No radio, nothing else talks to anything.

Now the problem that makes this a thesis and not just a product.

America does not make its own radios.

The companies that build the RF backbone of modern networks are all foreign: Nokia (Finland), Ericsson (Sweden), Samsung (Korea).

The Chinese ones, Huawei and ZTE, are banned outright on national-security grounds.

So the most powerful country on Earth, about to wire its economy, its defense and its AI into a wireless network, depends on other countries for the physical layer it runs on.

That is a strategic vulnerability.

Washington knows it.

That's the gap $AMPG fills.

AmpliTech is the only American company that designs and commercializes a 64T64R Massive MIMO O-RAN radio.

That's the highest-capacity radio configuration in the modern stack, and it's the physical hardware that open AI-RAN runs on.

Not the only one on Earth, Nokia and Ericsson make them too.

The only American one.

In a decade defined by re-shoring critical tech, that single word, American, is the whole point.

And this isn't a pitch deck.

It's already real.

It's deployed at Telus, a Tier-1 North American carrier, running on live Open RAN sites alongside Samsung.

It's a Strategic Partner in Open6G, the wireless hub funded by the US Department of Defense and run by Northeastern, sitting in the top partner tier right next to NVIDIA, Dell and Qualcomm.

Its radio was the physical unit in the world's first open-source Massive MIMO AI-RAN demo, running with NVIDIA's Aerial software.

And it was the only American-designed 64T64R radio to pass multi-vendor interoperability at the O-RAN ALLIANCE Global PlugFest.

Then look at who shows up on its customer wall: NVIDIA, Amazon, IBM, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, L3Harris, NASA.

You do not land defense primes by accident.

Those relationships take years of qualification before you're even in the room.

That's a moat you can't fake.

Now the fundamentals, because a thesis needs a business under it. 48% gross margins, up from 33%.

Debt-free.

$50M revenue guidance for the year (and they hit their prior guide, they don't have a habit of underdelivering).

And managament promised even more.

Real backlog, real LOIs.

This is a company that already makes money doing this, today, with the radio.

And stacked on top, for free, two pieces of optionality.

AI-RAN, where towers become intelligent edge nodes, the demo with NVIDIA points at exactly where this goes.

And quantum, where AMPG makes the cryogenic amplifiers superconducting quantum computers need for qubit readout (it's delivered proof-of-concept units to names like IBM and Google).

I'll be honest about both: optionality, not the core thesis. Cheap call options on top of a real business, not the reason to own it.

Here's the honest framing that actually makes this stronger, not weaker.

$AMPG is not a chokepoint nobody can replace.

AI runs without it.

Other radio makers exist.

I won't pretend it's irreplaceable, because it isn't.

What it is, is the sovereign alternative.

The American option in a layer the US increasingly refuses to outsource

That's a strategic preference backed by policy and funding, not a technical monopoly.

And strategically favored can re-rate a sub-$1B company just as hard as technically indispensable can.

And the timing isn't subtle.

The US just restricted its most advanced AI models from all foreign nationals, even allies.

When a country starts walling off its critical tech from its own friends, it tells you exactly how it's going to treat the physical layer its AI economy runs on.

It's going to want that made at home.

So in a map full of chokepoints and physical inputs, $AMPG is the layer that moves the signal, re-shored, certified, and American.

The screens get the attention.

The infrastructure gets the returns.

Not financial advice. I'm long $AMPG. Forget the headlines. Here's the infrastructure the next decade actually runs on, and the names building each layer 🧵👇

💡 PHOTONICS - light replaces copper
$CRDO · $SIVE · $AAOI
The interconnect bottleneck of AI. Every GPU cluster moving from electrons to light.

🔷 GLASS & OPTICAL MATERIALS - the substrate light runs on
$GLW · $LPTH · $LPKF
The fiber that carries the signal, the infrared optics that shape it, the glass cores going into advanced packaging.

⚙️ AI INFRASTRUCTURE - the compute buildout
$IREN · $NBIS · $DGXX
Power, data centers, neoclouds. The physical backbone of the AI economy.

📡 CONNECTIVITY & RF - moving the signal
$AMPG
Re-shored 5G and the RF signal chain. The certified domestic alternative for 5G networks, SATCOM, and defense.

🤖 PHYSICAL AI - intelligence that acts
$OUST · $AMBA · $KRKNF
The eyes, the brain, and the body. AI moving into the real world.

🛸 AUTONOMY & DRONES - machines that operate themselves
$ONDS · $AUR · $MRLN
Aerial drones, driverless freight, autonomous flight. The re-shored defense and logistics layer.

⚡ ENERGY & STORAGE - powering the buildout
$TE · $EOSE · $SPKL
Domestic solar, long-duration grid storage, and short-duration data-center backup. The full duration stack.

🪨 CRITICAL MINERALS - the hard constraints
$MP · $UAMY · $ASPI
Rare earths, antimony, nuclear fuel. The materials China controls that the West must re-shore.

🚀 SPACE - the orbital economy
$ASTS · $RKLB · $PL
Direct-to-cell satellites and the launch backbone.
The thesis in one line: own the chokepoints and physical inputs the digital economy can't function without.

The screens get the attention. text:
Quantum markets felt relatively sturdy today given the slip back into a fear market, rising tensions with Iran, and and overall selling backdrop. 

$QNT price action has certainly been disappointing. They'll have to backup their lofty valuation with continued progress. 

$IONQ continues getting knocked down and fighting back onto it's feet. 

$IBM is drifting lowers from it's recent new all-time high. A great opportunity to accumulate an A+ quantum name at a discount. 

$QUBT ends green showing the photonics story and sub-10 share price is attracting investors. 

$XNDU below $12 looks like an attractive risk-reward for long or short-term swings.

text:
Sharing message with you last night to my own trading group:

I’m going to start adding to the photonics theme, more on bigger drops from here

AAOI AXTI LITE COHR GLW

I expect them to stabilize first, then we likely bottom next week on Fed meeting as “buy the bad news kicks in”

But inflation needs to behave or fall in-line. Any significant beat is more downside after the Fed meeting.

Software names GOOGL NOW SNOW DDOG DOCN FSLY are buys for me next after photonics.

Finally, we monitor NVDA for final reversal/bottoming of entire AI trade 

CRWV neoclouds, heavy debt borrowers last to rally IMO

https://t.co/Iuhhj7kbHS

Optomechanical sensors enable exquisitely sensitive force measurements, with emerging applications across quantum technologies, standards, fundamental science, and engineering. Magnetometry is among the most promising applications, where chip-scale optomechanical sensors offer high sensitivity without the cryogenics or magnetic shielding required by competing technologies. However, a lack of compatibility with integrated photonics and electronics has posed a major barrier. Here, we introduce silicon-on-insulator optomechanical magnetometers to address this barrier.

Metasurface Revolutionizes Solar Polarimetry. A wafer of nanopatterned dielectric has replaced the mechanical heart of solar polarimetry, providing simultaneous full-Stokes snapshots of the Sun’s magnetic field. Engineers at UC San Diego, in collaboration with BAE Systems and NCAR solar physicists, have translated metasurface photonics into a flight-ready instrument. 

Their 6-millimeter metasurface polarization grating (MPG) diffracts sunlight into four angularly separated, high-contrast channels that encode the complete Stokes vector in one exposure. Traditional solar magnetographs require rotating analyzers and costly spacecraft stabilizers vulnerable to jitter. 

The metasurface replaces this mechanical system with a single passive optic carved from subwavelength dielectric pillars. These nanopillars impose precise phase and amplitude shifts on orthogonal polarization components, routing nearly 70% of the light into the four science orders, dumping less than 1% into the useless central zero order, and maintaining diattenuation above 98% across channels.

The entire polarization analysis collapses into one common optical path and one frozen instant, ideal for space missions where every gram and watt counts. At the Dunn Solar Telescope on Sacramento Peak, its 136-foot tower feeds light 228 feet underground to the compact SIMPol testbed. There, the 6-mm MPG sits in the pupil plane, dividing the wavefront into four pristine polarization portraits of the same sunspot. 

The data reveal interlocking magnetic loops of active regions with clarity matching established instruments, yet acquired without moving parts and at frame rates limited only by the detector.

text:
ho scritto un sacco di post su questi "photonics" cioè connessioni basate sulla luce o CPO che sono l'ultima moda della speculazione, tipo $Sivers, $Lite, Coherent $COHR Qui vedi finalmente un video di Nvidia che ne mostra un esempio.

 Nvidia video del suo switch di "ottica co-impacchettata" (CPO) con Lambda.

"La corsa all'IA non riguarda solo GPU più potenti, ma anche sprecare molta meno energia mentre quelle GPU comunicano tra loro.

Con l'ottica co-impacchettata (CPO), NVIDIA sta posizionando le parti di comunicazione basate sulla luce molto più vicino al chip di rete principale, invece di collocarli come moduli plug-in separati sul bordo dello switch.

"l'ottica co-impacchettata (CPO) si collega direttamente all'economia dei token. L'energia di rete è un sovraccarico: mantiene le GPU collegate ma non genera token. I guasti di rete sono anch'essi un sovraccarico: trasformano la capacità GPU provisionata in capacità inattiva. La CPO affronta entrambi riducendo il consumo energetico della rete e rimuovendo un'intera classe di componenti ottici pluggable dal fabric.

Un data center con 128.000 GPU che utilizza transceiver pluggable tradizionali richiede circa 655.000 moduli transceiver discreti attraverso il fabric di switching. Ognuno è un potenziale punto di guasto. La CPO rimuove completamente quella classe di componenti.

I carichi di lavoro agentici cambiano la pressione sulla rete. Una richiesta di inferenza tradizionale è relativamente autonoma. Una richiesta agentica può coinvolgere pianificazione, recupero, uso di tool, molteplici chiamate al modello e ragionamento di follow-up. Più dati che si muovono attraverso il cluster. Più punti in cui la latenza di rete o un guasto influenzano il risultato.

L'inferenza multi-agentica richiede un movimento dati elastico e resiliente, in modo che le GPU non aspettino i dati, mantenendo token al secondo e un tempo rapido al primo token."

Nvidia video del suo switch di "ottica co-impacchettata" (CPO) con Lambda. "La corsa all'IA non riguarda solo GPU più potenti, ma anche sprecare molta meno energia mentre quelle GPU comunicano tra loro. Con l'ottica co-impacchettata (CPO), NVIDIA sta posizionando le parti di comunicazione basate sulla luce molto più vicino al chip di rete principale, invece di collocarli come moduli plug-in separati sul bordo dello switch. "l'ottica co-impacchettata (CPO) si collega direttamente all'economia dei token. L'energia di rete è un sovraccarico: mantiene le GPU collegate ma non genera token. I guasti di rete sono anch'essi un sovraccarico: trasformano la capacità GPU provisionata in capacità inattiva. La CPO affronta entrambi riducendo il consumo energetico della rete e rimuovendo un'intera classe di componenti ottici pluggable dal fabric. Un data center con 128.000 GPU che utilizza transceiver pluggable tradizionali richiede circa 655.000 moduli transceiver discreti attraverso il fabric di switching. Ognuno è un potenziale punto di guasto. La CPO rimuove completamente quella classe di componenti. I carichi di lavoro agentici cambiano la pressione sulla rete. Una richiesta di inferenza tradizionale è relativamente autonoma. Una richiesta agentica può coinvolgere pianificazione, recupero, uso di tool, molteplici chiamate al modello e ragionamento di follow-up. Più dati che si muovono attraverso il cluster. Più punti in cui la latenza di rete o un guasto influenzano il risultato. L'inferenza multi-agentica richiede un movimento dati elastico e resiliente, in modo che le GPU non aspettino i dati, mantenendo token al secondo e un tempo rapido al primo token.""}}}, "quoted_status_result": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "core": {"user_results": {"result": {"__typename": "User", "affiliates_highlighted_label": {}, "avatar": {"image_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/profile_images/1816185267037859840/Fd18CH0v_normal.jpg"}, "core": {"created_at": "Wed Jun 25 22:38:54 +0000 2014", "name": "Rohan Paul", "screen_name": "rohanpaul_ai"}, "dm_permissions": {"can_dm": true}, "follow_request_sent": false, "has_graduated_access": true, "id": "VXNlcjoyNTg4MzQ1NDA4", "is_blue_verified": true, "legacy": {"default_profile": false, "default_profile_image": false, "description": "Compiling in real-time, the race towards AGI. The AI race is not only about stronger GPUs, but about wasting far less power while those GPUs talk to each other. With co-packaged optics (CPO), NVIDIA is putting the light-based communication parts much closer to the main networking chip, instead of placing them as separate plug-in modules at the edge of the switch. From NVIDIA's official blog on this "co-packaged optics (CPO) connects directly to the token economy. Network power is overhead: it keeps GPUs connected but doesn't generate tokens. Network failures are also overhead: they turn provisioned GPU capacity into idle capacity. CPO addresses both by reducing network power draw and removing a large class of pluggable optical components from the fabric. A 128,000-GPU data center using traditional pluggable transceivers requires roughly 655,000 discrete transceiver modules across the switching fabric. Each one is a potential failure point. CPO removes that component class entirely. Agentic workloads change the pressure on the network. A traditional inference request is relatively self-contained. An agentic request can involve planning, retrieval, tool use, multiple model calls, and follow-up reasoning. More data moving across the cluster. More points where network latency or failure affects the outcome. Multi-agentic inference needs elastic and resilient data movement, so GPUs are not waiting for data, while maintaining tokens per second and fast time to first token.""}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2064706125690462665"}}, "rest_id": "2064769648558756186", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "15500", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2064799993085719023", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1572", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
10 days of $SIVE catalysts. Let's take stock of where we are.

The catalysts:
→ June 2: GlobalFoundries silicon photonics reference design embedment. Stock +50-70%.

→ June 3: Ayar Labs joined NVIDIA's NVLink Fusion ecosystem. EU Tech Sovereignty Package adopted. ATH printed at 110 SEK.

→ June 5: JPMorgan disclosed 5.25% position via Finansinspektionen (transaction date June 2).

→ June 6: $YSS shipped 20+ Tranche 1 Transport Layer satellites for summer Falcon 9 launch.

→ June 8: ALL SPACE awarded $8.2M production order to $SIVE for 2027 Ka-band BFICs.

→ June 9: Anders Storm (former $SIVE CEO) flagged BlackRock and Fidelity as new institutional positions on Morningstar.

The structural conditions tightening underneath:

→ Nordea Bank raised bear-product margins by ~50% in 6 days, citing "poor liquidity in the securities lending market"

→ Three ETF issuers (REX, Defiance, Themes) have 2X LONG $SIVE products filed with SEC

→ MSCI Sweden Small Cap + OMX Stockholm Benchmark inclusions both effective

→ Short interest at 17% of free float

Today the stock is consolidating around 78-80 SEK after the all-time high of 110 SEK on June 3.

That's a ~27% pullback from peak. After a generational run.

This is what healthy consolidation looks like.

5 days until AGM (June 15). Board elections, dilution authorization vote, and management commentary on the Nasdaq NY listing timeline expected.

The chart is consolidating. The thesis is compounding.
$SIVE

text:
شركات النسخة الثانية من برنامج "صُمّم في السعودية بالذكاء الاصطناعي"

أعلنت كوالكوم، عبر شركتها التابعة Qualcomm Technologies International, Ltd.، وأرامكو السعودية، من خلال المختبر السعودي للابتكار المتسارع (aramcoSAIL)، وهيئة تنمية البحث والتطوير والابتكار @SaudiRDI ، وشركة هيوماين (HUMAIN)، عن الشركات الناشئة المختارة للمشاركة في النسخة الثانية من برنامج "صُمّم في السعودية بالذكاء الاصطناعي" (DISAI 2026).

ويهدف البرنامج إلى دعم الشركات الناشئة العاملة في مجال التقنيات العميقة والتي تطور حلولًا مبتكرة مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي في المملكة.

ويقدم البرنامج الدعم للشركات الناشئة ذات الإمكانات العالية، بدءًا من مرحلة الفكرة وصولًا إلى التسويق التجاري.

الشركات الناشئة المختارة في النسخة الثانية من البرنامج: 

1- شركة Circula: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 توفر منصة اقتصاد دائري مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لأتمتة استرداد النفايات الصناعية وتعزيز الامتثال البيئي.

2- شركة دقة إيه أي (Deqa AI): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة أتمتة مؤسسية تسهّل اتخاذ قرارات مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لتعزيز الكفاءة التشغيلية.

3- شركة ديجيتال بتروليوم (Digital Petroleum): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 طورت مستشعرًا صناعيًا مركزيًا مدعومًا بالذكاء الاصطناعي الطرفي يحل محل مستشعرات متعددة تقليدية ويُبسّط الرقمنة في إطار الجيل الرابع من الصناعة. 

4- شركة Electronic Photonics: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 توفر توأمًا رقميًا مروريًا مدعومًا بتقنية الليدار (LiDAR) والذكاء الاصطناعي الطرفي للتقاطعات الذكية وتحليلات التنقل.

5- شركة Finix Systems: شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم وحدة تحكم طرفية معيارية لإنترنت الأشياء الصناعي لتحديث المعدات القديمة وتمكين المراقبة الذكية.

6- شركة نوماس إيه آي (Nommas ai): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 طورت "عقلًا مصنعيًا ذكيًا" يتيح فحص الجودة والصيانة الاستباقية وتعزيز السلامة على المستوى الطرفي.

7- شركة سدم (SDM): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي لتشخيص الأمراض باستخدام التصوير الطبي للكشف المبكر.

8- شركة توكيد (Tawkeed): شركة ناشئة سعودية 🇸🇦 تقدم منصة ذكاء اصطناعي محلية لمعالجة الوثائق في الموقع، بالعربية أولًا، مع مراعاة متطلبات سيادة البيانات السعودية.

9- شركة Crosscall: شركة ناشئة فرنسية 🇫🇷 تقدم أجهزة تتبع قوية بتقنية الذكاء الاصطناعي للأشياء (AIoT) لشبكات 450 ميغاهرتز الصناعية وشبكات سلامة العمال.

10- شركة Dexabot: شركة ناشئة كندية 🇨🇦 متخصصة في الأمن الصناعي والمراقبة باستخدام الطائرات المسيّرة وأساطيلها.

text:
🚀Marvell Technology $MRVL stands out in amongst others in the AI infra:

Marvell is +300% past year as the market rewards their execution.

Not just another GPU play. Marvell dominates high-speed networking (800G/1.6T Ethernet, optical), custom ASICs/XPUs, and silicon photonics the critical “plumbing” powering massive AI data centers.

$NVDA invested $2B and brought them into NVLink Fusion. Jensen himself called them a potential next trillion-dollar company. Data center revenue exploding, custom silicon bookings strong, and FY2027 guidance raised again.

A name everyone should be watching closely.

MicroVision $MVIS is quietly flying under the radar (LiDAR?). 

The reverse split and need for cash are holding the share price back for now, but signing a long-term development agreement with 🤫 Caterpillar 🤫 should put the LiDAR sector on notice.

All of the hype, technology, talent and most importantly, partnerships, that Luminar acquired over the years since their SPAC debut, is now all being rolled up under MicroVision Inc.

Caterpillar ✅
Volvo
Gatik
+PlusAI
Mercedes 

These are all Luminar deals/relationship that MicroVision CEO, Glen DeVos will look to repair heading into 2027. 

And they even future proofed the portfolio by acquiring 1550nm FMCW player, Scantinel Photonics. 

$MVIS is a ticker to watch! 👀

Afternoon Market Update:

Chips are taking over the tape.

At midday $ORCL -11% felt like the big AI story. By 2:30 the entire semi complex is melting up and Oracle is yesterday's news. QQQ +2.5%.

🟢 $ARM +8.5% — Arm Holdings ($357B) leading the rally on BofA's $170B agentic AI call + a wave of analyst upgrades
 🟢 $MRVL +7.3% — Marvell ($241B) doubling its midday gain; "AI photonics" narrative taking hold
 🟢 $MU +6.5% — Micron ($1.08T) joining late after Wolfe sees more upside on a 223% YTD rally
 🟢 $INTC +6.5% — Intel ($576B) on a RARE BofA double upgrade (foundry + CPU outlook)
 🟢 $AMD +5.5% — Advanced Micro ($780B) ripping on the same BofA agentic note

🟢 $CPNG +13.2% — Coupang ($31B) still bouncing despite the $410M Korea fine
 🟢 $AAL +5.2% — American Airlines ($9B) extending into the afternoon

🔴 $ORCL -10.7% — Oracle ($517B) bleed continues but the tape moved past it; worst run in a quarter-century

🔝 Most active: $NVDA, $SMCI, $INTC. $SOXL +17.6% (3X semis ETF) telling you the magnitude.
