🇮🇳 *India Daybook – Stocks in News*

*Vascon Engineers:* Company has secured ₹347.43 crore RBI Quarters redevelopment project in Guwahati. (Positive)

*TCPL:* Company eyes 20%+ ebitda margin; growth to be driven by volumes and selective price hikes: Chandrasekaran. (Positive)

*BCPL Railway Infrastructure:* Company bags Rs. 13.1 crore south east central railway electrification project in Bilaspur division. (Positive)

*Krystal Integrated Services:* Company bags order worth Rs. 24.38 cr from the Office of Resident Commissioner, Maharashtra Sadan, New Delhi. (Positive)

*Dhruv Consultancy:* Company bags order worth Rs. 19.34 CR from South East Central Railway (Positive)

*Vantage Knowledge Academy Ltd.:* Company has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Kala Institute of Management Studies and Research (KIMSR), Mumbai.. (Positive)

*Univastu India Ltd:* Company has secured a significant project through a joint venture where it holds a 49% stake. (Positive)

*Bharti Hexacom:* Company gets relief as Bombay high court sets aside Rs 473.7 crore spectrum charge demand. (Positive)

*RMC Switchgears:* Company outlines FY27–FY29 growth strategy; bets on EPC, electrical products and pulsebox expansion. (Positive)

*Alfa Transformers Limited:* Company has been awarded a significant Letter of Award (LOA) from Madhya Gujarat Vij Company Ltd for the supply of various ratings of 11 KV transformers. (Positive)

*3i Infotech:* Purchase Order received from Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) worth ₹37.05 crores (exclusive of applicable tax). (Positive)

*Deccan Gold Mines:* Company enters definitive agreement for Logrosan Tungsten Project in Spain. (Positive)

*Power Companies:* May 2026 power demand rose 11% YoY (Positive)

*Textime stocks:* Government approves 96 Companies under round-III of Textile PLI Scheme (Positive)

*PPAP Automotive:* Partnership with Hutchison, a global leader in automotive sealing solutions. (Positive)

*JTL Industries:* Company has secured Rs. 26.7 Crore Order for GI Pipes in Himachal Pradesh (Positive)

*Sobhagya Mercantile:* Company bags Rs. 260.53 Cr Lift Irrigation Project in Maharashtra (Positive)

*CMR Green Technologies:* Goldman Sachs India Equity Portfolio bought 19.41 lk  shares at price Rs. 256.64 per  share. (Positive)

*NCC:* Sirisha Projects Promoter acquired 3.46 lk shares (Positive)

*Aluminum stocks:* Government Extends Anti-Dumping Duty on Aluminium Foil up to Dec 15, 2026 (Positive)

*Hindalco Industries:* Subsidiary Novelis successfully restarts the Oswego Hot Mill. (Positive)

*Izmo:* Company introduces India's first integrated silicon photonics packaging line to address fragmented workflows. (Positive)

*PNB:* Bank raises FCNR(B) deposit rates to 6.10%, expects USD 2.5 billion mobilisation (Neutral)

*SBIN:* Bank offers up to 6% interest on FCNR (USD) deposits (Neutral)

*Karur Vysya Bank:* Bank raises 3-5 year FCNR(B) deposit rates by 437 bps to 7%. (Neutral)

*IndiGrid Infrastructure Trust:* Company announces the merger of its Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) - Globus Steel, IndiGrid Solar-I (AP), & IndiGrid Solar-II (AP) with Godawari Green Energy Private Limited. (Neutral)

*RCOM:* Company has announced that its foreign step-down subsidiary, Reliance Communications (U.K.) Limited, has been officially dissolved. (Neutral)

*Cipla:* Company wants new-age medicines to contribute 10% of revenue in five years (Neutral)

*Sudarshan Pharma:* Sudarshan Industries Inc. (Sudarshan USA) incorporated as a wholly-owned subsidiary on 5th June 2026 in Dover, Delaware, USA. (Neutral)

*Rane Holdings':* Rane Holdings' subsidiary enters Power Purchase Agreement with Hexa Energy for solar power procurement at its Bawal, Haryana plant. (Neutral) 

*Juniper Hotels:* GeeCee Ventures acquired 50,000 equity shares in Juniper Hotels Ltd (JHL) at Rs. 198.27 per share. (Neutral) 

*Lenskart:* Platinum Jasmine likely to sell 2.3% stake in company via Block Deal: CNBC. (Neutral)

text:
The market should be cheering for domestic AI infrastructure champions like $AAOI, not trying to kill them every time volatility shows up.

This is not just a random optical stock.

This is about rebuilding critical Western supply chains.

Lasers.
Transceivers.
Optical networking.
Photonics.
AI data-center connectivity.
Domestic manufacturing capacity.
For years, the easy trade was to outsource everything to Asia, then short the weak U.S. / Western players and hide in the subsidized foreign winners.
That worked for a while.
But we already saw how that mindset backfired in solar, energy and now power-grid infrastructure.
You cannot complain about supply-chain dependence later if you refuse to support domestic capacity today.
That is why names like $AAOI, $INTC, $IQE, $XFAB, $MU, $WOLF, $SOI and $SIVE matter.

They are not all perfect companies.

They are not all low-risk stocks.

But they sit inside critical supply chains that the U.S. and Europe clearly want to secure.

CHIPS Act grants, reshoring incentives and strategic subsidies are not handed out randomly.

Governments are trying to rebuild the industrial base around AI, semiconductors, photonics, memory, power and advanced materials.

That is the real story.

If $AAOI can execute after reshoring more production back to America, the market may eventually stop treating it like a meme stock and start treating it like a strategic AI infrastructure supplier.

Instead of hoping these companies fail, maybe the smarter mindset is to understand why these supply-chain nodes exist in the first place.

The future AI stack will not only be built by software and GPUs.

It will also be built by lasers, optics, fabs, packaging, power and domestic manufacturing.

That is where I think the market is still underestimating the long-term opportunity.

This is not just a random optical stock. This is about rebuilding critical Western supply chains. Lasers. Transceivers. Optical networking. Photonics. AI data-center connectivity. Domestic manufacturing capacity. For years, the easy trade was to outsource everything to Asia, then short the weak U.S. / Western players and hide in the subsidized foreign winners. That worked for a while. But we already saw how that mindset backfired in solar, energy and now power-grid infrastructure. You cannot complain about supply-chain dependence later if you refuse to support domestic capacity today. That is why names like $AAOI, $INTC, $IQE, $XFAB, $MU, $WOLF, $SOI and $SIVE matter. They are not all perfect companies. They are not all low-risk stocks. But they sit inside critical supply chains that the U.S. and Europe clearly want to secure. CHIPS Act grants, reshoring incentives and strategic subsidies are not handed out randomly. Governments are trying to rebuild the industrial base around AI, semiconductors, photonics, memory, power and advanced materials. That is the real story. If $AAOI can execute after reshoring more production back to America, the market may eventually stop treating it like a meme stock and start treating it like a strategic AI infrastructure supplier. Instead of hoping these companies fail, maybe the smarter mindset is to understand why these supply-chain nodes exist in the first place. The future AI stack will not only be built by software and GPUs. It will also be built by lasers, optics, fabs, packaging, power and domestic manufacturing. text:
Lightwave Logic (LWLG) 公司急需失效分析工程師（FA Engineer）執行上述工作，核心原因在於其核心產品正處於從「實驗室研發」走向「大規模商業化量產」的關鍵轉折點。以下是該職位之所以至關重要的四大深層原因：

1. 確保「有機聚合物」的長期穩定性與壽命技術痛點：LWLG 的核心競爭力是「有機非線性聚合物（Perkinamine®）」。傳統光通訊晶片使用無機材料（如鈮酸鋰、磷化銦），而有機材料最常被市場質疑的就是耐熱性、耐光照與抗氧化等長期穩定性（Reliability）。FA 的任務：元件在長時間高溫、高雷射功率下失效時，FA 工程師必須查出究竟是材料變質（化學降解）、晶片封裝漏氣，還是電極氧化，藉此說服客戶其有機材料與傳統無機材料一樣耐用。

2. 跨越「實驗室到晶圓廠」的量產良率瓶頸技術痛點：LWLG 正在推動將其聚合物製程與現有的矽光子（Silicon Photonics）或 CMOS 晶圓廠（BEOL 後段製程）相容。在半導體廠代工時，製程中的化學清洗、高溫烘烤都可能對聚合物造成微觀損傷。FA 的任務：當晶圓產出發現不良品時，FA 必須判定是設計瑕疵、製程污染（如 SIMS、SEM 檢測出的雜質），還是合成化學製備時的分子缺陷，作為調整量產參數的依據。

3. 光通訊對「極致效能」的嚴苛要求技術痛點：人工智慧（AI）與資料中心爆發，要求光調變器具備極低電壓（低 \(V_{\pi }\)）、極低光損耗（Optical Loss）與超高頻寬（RF）。這些物理特性對結構微小的變化極度敏感。FA 的任務：當元件效能達不到預期（例如 \(V_{\pi }\) 電壓過高或光訊號過弱），FA 必須透過電光特性分析（如 Teng-Man 技術），找出是薄膜厚度不均、極化（Poled）不完全，還是波導結構粗糙導致的光散射。

4. 建立閉環修正機制（CAPA）以加速產品上市技術痛點：LWLG 的技術涉及多學科，從合成化學、製程、晶片結構到射頻設計。一旦出錯，各部門容易各自為政，找不到問題真正出在哪。FA 的任務：FA 工程師扮演「晶片法醫」的角色。用客觀的微觀檢測數據（AFM, TEM）說話，定義出失效是「材料問題」還是「加工問題」，驅動跨部門（如化學部與製程部）實施真正的矯正預防措施，避免研發資源浪費。

的核心競爭力是「有機非線性聚合物（Perkinamine®）」。傳統光通訊晶片使用無機材料（如鈮酸鋰、磷化銦），而有機材料最常被市場質疑的就是耐熱性、耐光照與抗氧化等長期穩定性（Reliability）。FA 的任務：元件在長時間高溫、高雷射功率下失效時，FA 工程師必須查出究竟是材料變質（化學降解）、晶片封裝漏氣，還是電極氧化，藉此說服客戶其有機材料與傳統無機材料一樣耐用。 2. 跨越「實驗室到晶圓廠」的量產良率瓶頸技術痛點：LWLG 正在推動將其聚合物製程與現有的矽光子（Silicon Photonics）或 CMOS 晶圓廠（BEOL 後段製程）相容。在半導體廠代工時，製程中的化學清洗、高溫烘烤都可能對聚合物造成微觀損傷。FA 的任務：當晶圓產出發現不良品時，FA 必須判定是設計瑕疵、製程污染（如 SIMS、SEM 檢測出的雜質），還是合成化學製備時的分子缺陷，作為調整量產參數的依據。 3. 光通訊對「極致效能」的嚴苛要求技術痛點：人工智慧（AI）與資料中心爆發，要求光調變器具備極低電壓（低 \(V_{\pi }\)）、極低光損耗（Optical Loss）與超高頻寬（RF）。這些物理特性對結構微小的變化極度敏感。FA 的任務：當元件效能達不到預期（例如 \(V_{\pi }\) 電壓過高或光訊號過弱），FA 必須透過電光特性分析（如 Teng-Man 技術），找出是薄膜厚度不均、極化（Poled）不完全，還是波導結構粗糙導致的光散射。 4. 建立閉環修正機制（CAPA）以加速產品上市技術痛點：LWLG 的技術涉及多學科，從合成化學、製程、晶片結構到射頻設計。一旦出錯，各部門容易各自為政，找不到問題真正出在哪。FA 的任務：FA 工程師扮演「晶片法醫」的角色。用客觀的微觀檢測數據（AFM, TEM）說話，定義出失效是「材料問題」還是「加工問題」，驅動跨部門（如化學部與製程部）實施真正的矯正預防措施，避免研發資源浪費。"}}}, "rest_id": "2065106818381308236", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1930", "state":
